This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Stomatanthes helenae include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Stomatanthes helenae include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma.
